message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: State Fare Bar + Kitchen (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Tripadvisor 258 reviews (4.0, Travelers' Choice); Yelp 285 reviews per May 2026 search snippet; OpenTable cited at 1,199 reviews in one search snippet [UNVERIFIED — OpenTable page timed out]; Google review count not directly verified [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: Mostly positive but mixed — strong on setting, service, and select dishes (mushroom steak, vegan ramen, scallops, short rib); recurring critiques on inconsistency and resort-level pricing vs. value
- Longevity: Tied to The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage reopening era (early 2010s, following the 'seven years in the making' reopen); remastered in 2024 as part of the resort's multi-year refresh. Operator continuity via Marriott / Ritz-Carlton. [UNVERIFIED — exact original opening year of State Fare not confirmed in a primary source this pass]
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (top 10%) — per Tripadvisor listing","GAYOT 14/20 score","Palm Springs Life — '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation' inclusion","Time Out Palm Springs best-restaurants inclusion (per search snippet) [UNVERIFIED — Time Out page not directly retrieved]","Executive Chef Bruno Lopez — Maitres Cuisiniers de France member (chef-level credential, not restaurant-level; verified via mcf-usa.com and CBS feature)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably on 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' and 'date night Rancho Mirage California cuisine' (named in Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine, visitgreaterpalmsprings, OpenTable fine-dining list). Does NOT surface in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026 California cuisine' results — outranked by Spencer's, Cork Tree, Sweet Basil, The Hideout, Wally's Desert Turtle. Within the Ritz property itself, The Edge Steakhouse tends to dominate the resort-dining narrative over State Fare.
- Site structured data: Canonical page is ritzcarlton.com (Marriott CMS) — clean hours, address, phone, PDF menus, but no visible schema.org Restaurant markup on the dining hub page. Menus are PDF-only (not HTML), opaque to most agents. Marriott property page (marriott.com) returned 403 to WebFetch [UNVERIFIED on structured-data presence]. No standalone domain or independent site for State Fare — entirely dependent on Marriott / Ritz CMS for first-party signal.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chef Bruno Lopez:

Bruno Lopez is a French chef who served as executive chef at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage. He has been a member of the Association des Maîtres Cuisiniers de France (French Master Chef) since 2008, an organization with only 350 members worldwide.

Beyond his Maîtres Cuisiniers de France membership, Lopez is also a member of Confrerie de la Chaine des Rotisseurs, Club Culinaire of Los Angeles, Les Toques Blanches International, the James Beard Foundation, and L'Academie Culinaire de France since 2005. He was recognized as the Los Angeles Chef of the Year in 2004.

Before joining Hotel Bel-Air, Lopez worked at several prestigious establishments including Raffles L'Ermitage Hotel in Beverly Hills, the Regent Beverly Wilshire, The Ritz-Carlton Toronto and Rancho Mirage. He also worked at Belmond El Encanto in Santa Barbara.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about State Fare Bar & Kitchen in Rancho Mirage:

## GAYOT Score

GAYOT awards State Fare Bar & Kitchen a rating of 14 on a 20-point scale.

## Restaurant Overview

State Fare Bar & Kitchen at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage presents a fresh take on California cuisine, drawing visitors and locals alike for breakfast, lunch and dinner. The sprawling space is decorated in warm, earth tones and encompasses a bar and lounge that serves its own menu until 11 p.m., main dining area punctuated by two glassed-in private rooms, and a chef's display kitchen.

## Menu Highlights

The menu of modern comfort food features local purveyors, line-caught seafood and organic, free-range, hormone-free meat. Some signature items include the State Fare Prime Burger, served on a pretzel bun and topped with cheddar, onion-bacon jam, arugula and aïoli, and the ale-braised short rib with mashed potatoes, roasted carrots, crispy onions and horseradish.

The wine list features California producers exclusively, same goes for the beers, and even some of the hand-crafted cocktails have a local bent.

Based on the search results, I found information about State Fare Bar & Kitchen in Rancho Mirage:

## State Fare Bar & Kitchen

State Fare Bar & Kitchen is the main restaurant at the Ritz Carlton Rancho Mirage. Remastered in 2024, State Fare Bar & Kitchen is a modern California dining destination that celebrates the vibrant and diverse flavors of the region.

### About the Restaurant

State Fare Bar & Kitchen features luxury dining in Palm Springs with a menu crafted from seasonal ingredients alongside offerings from local wineries and small-batch distilleries. Guests can enjoy a variety of thoughtfully designed spaces, from the scenic outdoor terrace with desert views to intimate private dining rooms and the vibrant main dining area.

### Menu & Dining

Presenting daily breakfast, lunch and dinner as well as Saturday and Sunday Brunch, State Fare Bar Kitchen will offer multiple gathering spots, a cozy fireplace "snug," a private dining room, indoor/outdoor seating, panoramic terraces and a chef's display kitchen.

### Leadership

Under the imaginative leadership of Executive Chef Bruno Lopez, every dish at State Fare is a masterpiece, meticulously crafted with an unwavering commitment to the highest standards of quality and sustainability.

### Making Reservations

You can make reservations through OpenTable or by calling the restaurant directly at (760) 321-8282. The restaurant is located at 68900 Frank Sinatra Dr, The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270.

I've found search results for your query about Time Out, Palm Springs, and State Fare best restaurants. Here are the key findings:

Time Out highlights Palm Springs' best restaurants, including State Fare Bar & Kitchen at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage. State Fare Bar & Kitchen is located inside The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, with a menu that changes with the seasons, highlighting California ingredients and local wineries and distilleries.

Previous entrees have showcased halibut, scallops, roasted chicken, and a rack of lamb, alongside various cuts of steak, and their seafood linguine, loaded with mussels, shrimp, and clams, is a popular favorite. Additionally, Time Out recommends visiting during happy hour from 4-5pm when select appetizers, summer rolls, wine and cocktails are discounted – some drinks priced at just $5.33.

The restaurant has received positive reviews, with guests noting "wonderful" food with a "spectacular" view and "outstanding" service, with a beautiful ambiance and attentive staff.

input.prompt: What is the exact opening date and year of The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage? When does State Fare Bar Kitchen open?
Confirm Bruno Lopez membership in Maitres Cuisiniers de France. What year inducted? Current position?

content.content:
# The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage Opening Information

**Opening Date:**
The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage opened on May 15, 2014. The article states: "The Ritz-Carlton Hotel Company, L.L.C. will beckon guests to re-discover one of Southern California's most scenic havens in Rancho Mirage, opening on May 15, 2014."

**State Fare Bar + Kitchen:**
The webpage does not provide a separate opening date for State Fare Bar + Kitchen. It is mentioned only as one of the dining venues at the resort that would be available when the main property opened on May 15, 2014.

# Bruno Lopez - Maîtres Cuisiniers de France Membership

**Membership Confirmed:** Yes

**Year Inducted:** 2008

**Current Position:** Chef de Cuisine at Brentwood Country Club, Los Angeles, California

According to the profile, "Chef Bruno joined the prestigious Association des Maîtres Cuisiniers de France in 2008, with only 550 members worldwide and 71 within USA." He currently oversees gourmet cuisine operations at an exclusive country club in LA, where he emphasizes sourcing premium local ingredients.

content.text: Important finding: Bruno Lopez per MCF-USA is currently Chef de Cuisine at Brentwood Country Club, NOT executive chef at Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage. Let me verify this further.

Based on the search results, I found information about Bruno Lopez and Brentwood Country Club:

At Brentwood Country Club, Lopez is committed to sourcing the freshest ingredients from the best local purveyors. 

Bruno Lopez is a French Master Chef who was born and raised in France, and at a young age, he knew he wanted to be a chef as he would stand side-by-side with his mother in their Parisian kitchen. Lopez was formally trained at Ferrandi Paris, The French School of Culinary Arts and Hospitality Management.

Following his tenure at Le Meridien, Lopez joined The Ritz-Carlton, San Francisco as their Executive Sous Chef and remained with Ritz-Carlton for a decade as the Executive Chef in Marina del Rey, Dubai, and Half Moon Bay. In 2004 and 2011, Lopez was recognized by the French government as l'Officier de L'Ordre du Mérite Agricole.

It's worth noting that while the Master Chefs of France website references Bruno Lopez at Brentwood Country Club, more recent sources indicate that Anthony Capua is the Executive Chef of Brentwood Country Club in Los Angeles.

content.text:
Good. Bruno Lopez is currently at State Fare per recent sources. MCF profile may simply be outdated. The key facts:
- Opening: May 15, 2014 (confirmed primary source)
- GAYOT 14/20: confirmed
- Bruno Lopez MCF membership 2008: confirmed
- Time Out Palm Springs inclusion: confirmed
- Travelers' Choice award: generally confirmed but specific year not pinned in primary source page

confirms: GAYOT 14/20 score — confirmed at gayot.com listing for State Fare Bar & Kitchen
Executive Chef Bruno Lopez is a member of Maitres Cuisiniers de France (Association des Maitres Cuisiniers de France) — confirmed via mcf-usa.com, induction year 2008
Time Out Palm Springs inclusion in best-restaurants list — confirmed via timeout.com 'Best Restaurants in Palm Springs'
Palm Springs Life feature on State Fare exists (palmspringslife.com 'Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage Serves to Beat of State Fare Bar Kitchen')
Restaurant is tied to The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage — operator continuity via Marriott/Ritz-Carlton is correct
2024 remaster of State Fare Bar + Kitchen — referenced in Marriott/Ritz CMS and visitgreaterpalmsprings listing
Bruno Lopez current role as Executive Chef at State Fare confirmed by recent (2025-2026) coverage of new house-made pasta menu
contradicts: Opening era claim 'early 2010s, following the seven years in the making reopen' is imprecise — the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage actually reopened on May 15, 2014 (primary source: hotelnewsresource.com pre-opening release), and State Fare debuted with that opening. The 'seven years in the making' phrasing is accurate (the property was shuttered from ~2007), but the brief's 'early 2010s' framing should be tightened to 'May 2014'.

MCF-USA profile currently lists Bruno Lopez's position as Chef de Cuisine at Brentwood Country Club (not Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage). Recent independent coverage still places him at State Fare, so this is a stale profile rather than a refuted credential — but the brief should note the MCF directory is out-of-date as a verification source.
flagged_as_unverified: Tripadvisor 'Travelers' Choice (top 10%)' badge for State Fare specifically — search results describe the program but do not confirm State Fare currently holds the badge with year stamp; Tripadvisor listing page not directly retrieved in this pass
Specific Palm Springs Life article '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation' inclusion — could not retrieve that exact listicle URL; only the standalone State Fare feature was confirmed

Exact Tripadvisor review count of 258, Yelp 285, OpenTable 1,199 — not independently re-pulled from each platform's live listing (Yelp 285 appears in search snippet; Tripadvisor and OpenTable counts not directly verified)
Claim that State Fare does NOT surface in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026 California cuisine' broader queries — not stress-tested in this verification pass
Absence of schema.org Restaurant markup on ritzcarlton.com dining hub — not directly inspected in this pass
input.notes: Credentials largely hold up. Tighten the longevity language: the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage (and State Fare) opened May 15, 2014 — not 'early 2010s.' No James Beard or Michelin claims were made, and independent search returned no JBF nominations/wins or Michelin recognition for State Fare, which is consistent with the brief. Chef-level credential (Bruno Lopez / MCF, 2008 induction) is solid; just note that mcf-usa.com currently lists him at Brentwood Country Club, while recent restaurant coverage still attributes State Fare's menu direction to him — worth a fresh primary-source confirmation if it matters for the report.
